source: Hand-curated provider metadata from first-party sources and OpenRouter provider metadata (refreshed 2026-07-12)
notes: eu_hosted = the provider runs inference on EU-located servers (the explicit set the product cares about: the hyperscalers' EU regions + EU-native providers). non_us = the operating company is not headquartered in the United States. country is the company HQ. These flags drive the global 'EU hosted' and 'Non-US provider' filters. Keyed by exact provider NAME. OpenRouter aliases do not inherit region guarantees from similarly named first-party catalogs: for example Amazon Bedrock and Azure remain non-EU unless their route itself proves an EU region. hyperscaler=true marks the big-cloud managed catalogs/routes.

AWS Bedrock.note: EU residency via the eu. cross-region Geo profile; ZDR by default. US parent (CLOUD Act).

Azure AI Foundry.note: EU Data Boundary / EU Data Zone. NOTE: Fireworks-hosted models (Kimi/GLM/MiniMax) are excluded from the EU boundary (US-served).

Google Vertex AI.note: EU residency via europe-west / EU multi-region; never the global endpoint. US parent.

Nebius.note: EU-capable Netherlands provider with Finland/France infrastructure, opt-in ZDR + no-training, ISO 27001/27701 and SOC 2 II. The serverless catalog is mixed-region: every offer's eu-north1/us-central1/uk-south1 region must be checked separately.

Inceptron.note: Sweden-based; OpenRouter metadata reports its serving datacenter in Finland (FI). Zero-retention; current catalog serves GLM 5.2, Kimi K2.6/K2.7 Code, and MiniMax M2.5.

Scaleway.note: FR-sovereign Generative APIs, current serverless pricing region Paris; ZDR by default. Serves GLM 5.2 plus Llama/Qwen/Mistral/Gemma/gpt-oss/Holo open models; no current DeepSeek or MiniMax endpoint.

IONOS.note: DE-sovereign OpenAI-compatible AI Model Hub. Serves Llama/Mistral/gpt-oss and Qwen3-Coder-Next 80B (open models only).

TensorX.note: EU-sovereign serverless per-token API (Dublin HQ, 3 EU data-centre regions, isolated from US hyperscalers). Zero data retention; GDPR/EU AI Act, ISO 27001-ready. Serves GLM/Kimi/DeepSeek/MiniMax/Qwen/gpt-oss — incl. the SOTA Chinese coding models, in-EU.

OVHcloud.note: French sovereign-cloud provider. First-party AI Endpoints material states that inference data is hosted in Europe, the service runs on OVHcloud infrastructure, and prompts use zero retention apart from billing data. The 2026-07-12 catalog has 9 priced text-generating LLM/VLM offers.

STACKIT.note: German sovereign cloud. All current Shared Chat models use the eu01 OpenAI-compatible endpoint; the official price list identifies EU01 as Germany South. Six chat models were supported on 2026-07-12.

T-Systems LLM Hub.note: AI Foundation Services catalog is mixed hosting: open models marked T-Cloud are sovereign Germany inference, while proprietary models are routed to Azure or GCP EU regions and retain upstream-provider terms. Inspect each offer's hosting_class; preview rows are not generally priced production offers.

NextBit.note: OpenRouter metadata reports headquarters ES and datacenters ES; EU-hosted OpenRouter endpoints, including DeepSeek V4 Flash.

TrustedRouter.note: EU-based, security-focused router — still being launched, not production-ready yet.

Amazon Bedrock.note: Exact OpenRouter provider name. EU-capable only on endpoints explicitly tagged amazon-bedrock/eu-west-1; global routes do not inherit EU residency.

Ambient.note: OpenRouter's provider API currently publishes neither headquarters nor datacenters. Conservatively excluded from EU-hosted and non-US filters pending first-party evidence.

Azure.note: Exact OpenRouter provider name. EU-capable only on endpoints explicitly tagged azure/eu or azure/swedencentral; global routes do not inherit EU residency.

Darkbloom.note: Operated by Eigen Labs, Inc. under California law. Public-alpha Apple-hardware attestation is not evidence of EU residency or confidential inference equivalent to the app's TEE flag; current terms also state the absence of end-to-end encryption.

Decart.note: OpenRouter provider metadata reports headquarters US and datacenters US.

Inflection.note: US company; OpenRouter exposes no guaranteed EU serving datacenter for this route.

Mancer 2.note: Mancer's first-party privacy policy identifies Sunlit Software Inc. and states that the platform is hosted in the United States.

ModelRun.note: OpenRouter provider metadata reports headquarters US and datacenters US.

Morph.note: OpenRouter provider metadata reports headquarters US and datacenters US.

OpenInference.note: OpenRouter provider metadata reports headquarters US and no guaranteed EU datacenter.

Perceptron.note: OpenRouter provider metadata reports headquarters US and no guaranteed EU datacenter.

Poolside.note: Poolside, Inc. is US-founded/US-headquartered. Its privacy material permits variable infrastructure locations; the OpenRouter route provides no EU residency guarantee.

Relace.note: Relace's first-party privacy policy identifies Squack Inc. at 130 Bush Street, San Francisco. No EU serving guarantee is published for the OpenRouter route.

xAI.note: OpenRouter provider metadata reports headquarters US and no guaranteed EU datacenter.

Google.note: Exact OpenRouter provider name. EU-capable only on endpoints explicitly tagged google-vertex/eu or google-vertex/europe; global routes do not inherit EU residency.

DeepInfra.note: Single global endpoint, US data centers (per own privacy policy); strong ZDR + SOC2/ISO27001 but no self-serve EU region. (One 3rd-party blog claims EU GPUs — unconfirmed, contradicted by DeepInfra's own docs.)

Together.note: EU footprint (Sweden, since Sep 2025) is GPU clusters / dedicated endpoints only; the per-token serverless API has no EU region selector and runs from US capacity. US HQ (CLOUD Act). Not an EU serverless price source.

Fireworks.note: EU only via dedicated (Frankfurt/Iceland) / BYOC GPU deployments, not serverless.

Sakana AI.note: Tokyo-headquartered AI lab; OpenRouter serving location is global/unspecified, so non-US company filtering applies but EU hosting does not.

Parasail.note: US-HQ (San Mateo, CA). Single global auto-routed serverless endpoint, no EU region pin; region handling is a paid Dedicated/sales feature only.

Cloudflare.note: Self-serve Workers AI uses global anycast to nearest GPU, no EU pinning; EU/region-locked inference is Enterprise-only via Data Localization Suite / Custom Regions ("not yet self-serve").

Venice.note: Privacy-first / no-logs but US (per own privacy policy the Service is US-hosted); compute is a global decentralized GPU pool (Akash/Phala/etc.) with no selectable/guaranteed EU geography. No-logs != EU residency.

Novita.note: US-HQ, AWS-backed global per-token API; single endpoint api.novita.ai, no region param. GDPR-compliance claims only, no EU region for self-serve serverless.

AtlasCloud.note: Public per-token API is US/global with no region selection; an eu-west region exists only for deploy-your-own dedicated serverless endpoints (out of scope).

WandB.note: CoreWeave-powered serverless is US-only (multi-tenant on GCP North America, only coreweave-us storage); EU exists solely for sales-gated Dedicated Cloud. CoreWeave (US) owned.

Phala.note: TEE/confidential but US/India nodes, not EU-resident.

Chutes.note: TEE models available; node location not EU-pinned.

DigitalOcean.note: Serverless inference is US-only (single global endpoint inference.do-ai.run, no region param); EU regions run only NA-restricted Dedicated/Agent products. DO states GDPR EU inference regions are roadmap, not shipped (re-check 2026-H2).

Friendli.note: FriendliAI is headquartered at 303 Twin Dolphin Drive, Redwood City, California; Seoul is an additional hub, not company HQ. OpenRouter likewise reports headquarters US. ZDR by default.

AionLabs.note: OpenRouter metadata reports headquarters IL and no guaranteed EU datacenter.

Mistral.note: French first-party API; data is hosted in the EU by default unless the explicit US endpoint is selected. Serves Mistral's own Open, Premier, and Labs models.
